commentary: Olive blossom peaked late this year across the Subbética. Early fruit set looks strong where growers held back on nitrogen. Field notes from this week's grove visits in comments.
Looking for two more single-estate Picual producers in Córdoba for our autumn premium range. Quality over volume. Message me if you press your own oil.
Our 2025 harvest pool earned the highest average sensory scores in the cooperative's history. Thank you to every member grove. Autumn general meeting set for 22 October.

East grove is in year two of the three-year organic conversion. 12 hectares, all Picual. Final EU inspection is set for spring 2027. Cover crop between the rows is holding the soil through the dry months and we have cut herbicide to zero. Records for the audit are kept by hand in the field diary.
Just back from sourcing trips in Andalusia. The best Spanish EVOO arriving in the UK this year is coming from small estates that mill within hours of harvest. Worth seeking out.
Reminder to member growers: precision drip plus a well-kept field diary is what gets you through a dry summer and an organic audit. Both pay off long before harvest.
Tasted this year's early Picual samples from the Subbetica. Green, peppery, alive. If you produce single-estate oil and mill same-day, my autumn range still has two slots.
Our shared analytics cluster now runs yield forecasts for every member grove. Data in, better irrigation and harvest decisions out. Demo at the 22 October general meeting.
Cover crops between olive rows are not a cost, they are an investment. Better water retention, more biodiversity, healthier soil. Field results from the Subbetica groves this season are clear.
Summer tasting season has begun at the estate. Olive oil flights, a dawn birdwatching walk, and breakfast in the grove. Soo-Jin here, booking visits through the newsletter.
Allocations for premium Spanish EVOO are tightening for autumn. The single-estate Picual and Hojiblanca from the Baena DOP region remain our buyers' favourites. Reserve early.
Proud of our member growers entering year two of organic transition. The east-grove conversions are showing the kind of soil recovery that gets you through a hot, dry Andalusian summer.
Sourcing trip to Cordoba next month. Looking to add one more organic-in-transition Picual estate to the Geneve range. Quality and traceability first. Send me your tech sheets.
We will be at the Jaen olive oil fair on 21 November with the new Miles harvest. First public pour of this year's pressing. Come find the Finca Miles table.
Replanted the windbreak hedge on the north boundary of the east grove this spring. Six months on, it has taken hold. Buffer zone is solid for the final organic inspection.
Drip irrigation season is here. We are mapping precision watering for olive groves across Cordoba ahead of the October installation window. Book your site survey now.
Restocking the larder for autumn with single-estate Andalusian oils. The best ones tell you the grove, the variety, and the press date. Anything less is just olive oil.
Our quarterly tasting newsletter goes out next week. Harvest countdown, a new Arbequina release, and dates for the autumn open days at the estate. Subscribe through the Finca Miles site.
